note: First-pass CTM reflections for the anchor batch. DRAFTS, written to be reacted to and rewritten, not shipped as-is. Each is confidence 'personal' (an honest ministry reflection, not claimed as consensus) per docs/04-moderation-design.md. Tim owns these words; edit freely in the review tool.

reflections.JHN.3.16-17: "Gave" is the hardest word here to receive. We spend our lives trying to earn a place, to prove we belong, to pay something back; here God simply gives, before we ask and before we deserve it. On the farm we learn the harvest was never ours to manufacture, only to tend what was already given.
reflections.PSA.23.1-6: A shepherd is not a manager; he lives with the sheep, walks the same ground, sleeps in the same weather. The line does not promise we will never want; it promises we will not want for Him. Formation is learning to be led by someone who stays.
reflections.JER.29.11-14: These words were written to exiles, not to people whose lives were going well; the future and the hope were promised in the middle of the loss, not instead of it. That order matters. God's plans rarely spare us the wilderness; they meet us in it.
reflections.ROM.8.28-39: Not conquerors who avoided the trouble, but more than conquerors through it; the trouble is named first, and named plainly. A faith that only works when life is easy has not met this verse yet. We are carried, not spared.
reflections.PHP.4.4-9: Paul does not say stop feeling anxious; he says do something with it, item by item, with thanks mixed in. Anxiety left in the chest becomes dread; anxiety handed over becomes peace. The handing over is the work.
reflections.ISA.40.28-31: Waiting is not passive here; it is the strength itself. We are told strength means pushing harder, and this verse says the opposite: the ones who last are the ones who learned to wait on God rather than on their own reserves.
reflections.PRO.3.5-8: Leaning not on your own understanding does not mean thinking less; it means trusting Someone more than your read of the situation. Most of us lean hardest on ourselves exactly when we are most afraid. This is the invitation to stop.
reflections.MAT.11.28-30: The invitation is to the weary and burdened, not the rested and ready; you do not have to get yourself together first. The rest here is not the absence of work but a different yoke, one that fits. Come as you are is not a slogan; it is the entry requirement.
reflections.1CO.13.1-13: Patient and kind come first, before any of the grand words, because they are the ones that cost daily. It is easy to feel love and hard to be patient at hour fourteen. This is love with its work clothes on.
reflections.GAL.5.22-25: Fruit is not manufactured; it grows from what the tree is joined to. The list is not a set of tasks to achieve but evidence of a life rooted in the Spirit. You do not strain to produce fruit; you abide, and it comes.
reflections.ROM.12.1-2: Conformed happens by default; transformed takes a renewed mind, which takes time and repetition. We are shaped by whatever we soak in, for better or worse. Formation is choosing what shapes you before it shapes you without asking.
reflections.MAT.6.9-15: Your will be done is the hardest line in the prayer, because it means loosening our grip on our own. To pray it honestly is to want God's kingdom more than our own comfort. Most of us have to pray it many times before we mean it.
reflections.LAM.3.21-26: This is said by a man in ruins; the faithfulness is great precisely because everything else has failed. New every morning means we do not have to carry yesterday's supply. Mercy does not stockpile; it arrives fresh, daily, like bread.
reflections.HEB.11.1-3: Faith is not certainty about what we can see; it is assurance about what we cannot. That is why it is called faith and not sight. It does not remove the unseen; it lets us stand on it anyway.
reflections.MIC.6.6-8: Not sacrifices, not spectacle, just this: to act justly, to love mercy, to walk humbly. The requirement is smaller than we fear and harder than we hope. A whole life fits inside those three.
reflections.2CO.12.7-10: Paul asked three times for the thorn to go, and the answer was not removal but sufficiency. Grace did not fix him; it held him. Weakness turned out to be the place strength gets in.
reflections.EPH.2.8-10: By grace, through faith, not from yourselves; every clause closes another door on earning it. This is the hardest gift for capable people to receive, because there is nothing to do but take it. We are saved by what was done, not by what we manage.
